title: Criminal Force
content: Using force intentionally on another without consent for a wrongful purpose is criminal force.

title: Assault
content: Making any gesture or preparing to apply force, causing another to apprehend imminent harm, is assault.

title: Punishment for Assault
content: Assault or use of criminal force without grave provocation is punishable with imprisonment up to 3 months or fine.

title: Assault on Public Servant
content: Assaulting or using force against a public servant lawfully performing duty is punishable with up to 2 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Assault to Dishonor a Person
content: Intentionally assaulting someone to insult or humiliate them (e.g., pulling clothing) is punishable with up to 2 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Force to Commit Theft
content: Using force or threat to steal anything worn or carried by a person is an offence.

title: Hurt
content: Causing bodily pain, disease, or infirmity to a person is hurt.

title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt
content: Intentionally or knowingly causing hurt to another person; punishable with up to 1 year imprisonment or fine.

title: Theft
content: Dishonestly moving any movable property out of another’s possession without consent is theft; punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment (up to 5 years on repeat).

title: Dishonest Misappropriation
content: Dishonestly converting someone else’s movable property to one’s own use, knowing it was not yours, is misappropriation; punishable with 6 months to 2 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Cheating
content: Fraudulently inducing a person to deliver property or act to their harm by deceit is cheating; punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Criminal Trespass
content: Entering or remaining on someone’s property with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate or annoy is criminal trespass; violating a dwelling is house-trespass.

title: Criminal Intimidation
content: Threatening to harm a person’s body, reputation or property (or someone they care about) with intent to cause alarm or to coerce them is criminal intimidation; punishable with up to 2 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Defamation
content: Making or publishing an imputation intended to harm a person’s reputation is defamation, unless it falls under a valid exception (e.g., truth for public good).

title: Receiving Stolen Digital Property
content: Dishonestly receiving or retaining stolen computer resources or communication devices, knowing they are stolen, is punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Identity Theft
content: Fraudulently using another’s electronic signature, password or unique ID to gain benefit is punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Cyber Cheating by Personation
content: Using a computer resource to cheat by personation is punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Violation of Privacy
content: Intentionally capturing, publishing or transmitting the image of a person’s private area without consent (thereby violating privacy) is punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Data Protection Failure
content: If a body corporate is negligent in securing personal data and causes wrongful loss to a person, it must compensate the affected person.

title: Breach of Confidentiality by Official
content: A person with privileged access who discloses electronic records without consent is punishable with up to 2 years imprisonment or fine.

title: Deficiency of Service
content: Any fault, shortcoming or inadequacy in the quality or performance of a service that a provider is bound to maintain by law or contract is a deficiency.

title: District Commission Jurisdiction
content: District Consumer Commission handles complaints where value of goods/services and compensation claimed does not exceed ₹1 crore.

title: State Commission Jurisdiction
content: State Consumer Commission handles complaints where value of goods/services and compensation claimed exceeds ₹1 crore but is up to ₹10 crore.

title: National Commission Jurisdiction
content: National Consumer Commission handles complaints where value of goods/services and compensation claimed exceeds ₹10 crore.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: GST Registration Threshold
content: Any supplier of goods or services with aggregate turnover exceeding ₹20 lakh (or ₹10 lakh in special category states) in a financial year must register under GST.

title: Cheque Dishonour
content: Dishonour of a cheque (due to insufficient funds or other valid reasons) intended to discharge any debt is punishable; drawer faces imprisonment or fine.

title: FIR Registration
content: Every information about a cognizable offence given to a police officer must be written down and signed (FIR). The complainant gets a copy free of cost.

title: Maintenance of Wife/Children
content: If a person neglects his wife or minor children who cannot maintain themselves, a magistrate can order monthly maintenance from him.

title: Drunken Driving
content: Driving a motor vehicle with alcohol above prescribed limit is punishable with up to 6 months imprisonment or ₹1,000 fine (up to 2 years or ₹1 lakh on repeat offence).

title: Duty in Emergencies
content: A driver must report any accident to the police and assist in shifting or repairing the vehicle, or face penalties.

title: Central Govt. Powers
content: The central government may take measures necessary for protecting or improving environmental quality and preventing pollution.

act: Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
title: Consent Requirement
content: No industrial plant can be established or operated without prior consent from the State Pollution Control Board.

title: Harmful Weapons
content: Carrying or using lethal weapons (swords, knives etc.) that risk public safety is an offence.

title: Disobedience by Public Servant
content: A public servant disobeying legal orders or directions can be punished as prescribed by law.

title: Sedition
content: Any act or speech inciting disaffection toward the government is punishable, but mere criticism of policies is not sedition.

title: Electronic Records Evidence
content: Digital records (emails, electronic files) are admissible as evidence if conditions of Sec 65B are fulfilled.

title: Gratuity Eligibility
content: An employee who has worked at least 5 years is entitled to gratuity on termination of service.

title: Bonus Eligibility
content: An employee who has worked at least 30 days in a year is eligible for a statutory bonus.

title: Cleanliness
content: Every factory must be kept clean and free of effluvia so as to not be injurious to workers’ health.

title: Fixation of Minimum Wages
content: Governments fix minimum rates of wages for different employments to ensure fair pay.

title: Working Hours
content: Shops cannot open before 8 AM or remain open beyond 7 PM on normal working days without special permission.

title: Contribution to Provident Fund
content: Employers and employees each contribute a percentage of wages to the employee’s Provident Fund.

title: ESI Contribution
content: Employers and employees contribute a percentage of wages to the ESI scheme for health insurance benefits.

title: Green Nodal Agency
content: Central Pollution Control Board is the nodal agency to monitor enforcement of environmental laws before the NGT.

title: Bar of Other Courts
content: No court (other than the NGT) can entertain matters that fall under the jurisdiction of the NGT.

title: Anti-Competitive Agreements
content: Agreements or practices that harm competition (like price fixing) are prohibited.

title: Abuse of Dominance
content: Any abuse of dominant position by enterprises (like predatory pricing) is prohibited.

title: Proactive Disclosure
content: Public authorities must proactively publish information about their functions, decisions, and finances.

title: Making RTI Request
content: Any citizen can request information from a public authority by writing to the Public Information Officer (PIO).

title: Wrongful Communication
content: Wrongful communication, sharing or publication of classified official information is an offence.

title: What is a Contract
content: An agreement with free consent, lawful consideration and lawful object is a contract.

title: Compensation for Breach
content: On breach of contract, the injured party can claim compensation for any loss or damage caused.

title: Sale of Goods
content: Sale is a contract where the seller transfers or agrees to transfer property in goods to the buyer for a price.

title: Title to Goods
content: A buyer gets title to goods subject to any lien for charges owed on them.

title: Facts in Issue
content: Facts which are in dispute between parties and to which the law applied, are called facts in issue.

title: Title and Extent
content: Certain specified establishments are exempt from provisions of specified labour laws.

title: Prohibition of Trade
content: Hunting or trade in endangered wild animals or their derivatives is prohibited.

title: Penalties
content: Violating the Act’s wildlife protection provisions attracts imprisonment and fines.

title: Bribery by Public Servant
content: A public servant taking gratification (other than legal remuneration) for doing or forbearing official action is punishable.

title: Criminal Misconduct
content: A public servant is guilty of misconduct if they obtain a valuable thing with corrupt intent or dishonestly use their office to cause loss to the government.

title: Sexual Harassment at Work
content: All workplaces must prevent and address sexual harassment of women.

title: Penalties
content: Breaches of provisions for preventing sexual harassment are punishable with fines.

title: Bonus Rate
content: Employers must pay a minimum of 8.33% and a maximum of 20% of salary/wages as bonus, subject to eligibility.

title: State Commission Jurisdiction
content: State Commission handles disputes where the claim exceeds ₹1 crore but is up to ₹10 crores.

title: New Income Tax Regime
content: Defines new tax rates and rebates for individual and HUF taxpayers who opt for the lower slab rates.

title: Cash Transaction Limit
content: Receiving more than ₹2 lakh in cash in a single day (without banking it) is subject to penalty.

title: Notice of Dishonour
content: Notice of dishonour must be given to the party at whose request a bill was drawn, indicating the cause of dishonour.

title: Board Composition
content: Every company must have a Board of Directors that includes at least one woman director.

title: Restrictions on Share Transfer
content: Transfer of shares may be restricted by company regulations and cannot be transferred without approval as per articles.

title: Commencement of Limitation
content: The limitation period for legal claims begins when the right to sue first accrues to the aggrieved party.

title: Welfare of Workers
content: Factories employing more than 500 workers must provide welfare facilities like canteens, rest shelters, and creches.

title: Welfare Officer
content: Factories with more than 1000 workers must appoint one or more welfare officers.

title: Wage Period
content: Wages must be paid within a prescribed period after the end of each wage period (maximum 1 month for pieceworkers, 15 days for others).

title: Equal Pay for Men and Women
content: Employers must pay equal remuneration to male and female workers for the same work or work of a similar nature.

title: Fixation of Minimum Rates
content: State governments fix or revise minimum rates of wages based on cost of living and necessity among workers.

title: Absolute Grounds for Refusal
content: Trademarks that are devoid of distinctiveness, descriptive or generic cannot be registered.

title: Non-Patentable Inventions
content: A new form of a known substance without enhanced efficacy or obvious changes are not patentable.

title: Appointment of Arbitrators
content: Parties to an arbitration can agree on arbitrators; failing that, the court can appoint arbitrators on application.

title: Penalty for Contravention
content: Contravention of any FEMA regulation by a person is an offence punishable by fine and/or imprisonment.
